title: 10 best herbal teas for nail fungus

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Germander teas helps with nail fungus because it contains compounds like terpinen-4-ol, beta-caryophyllene, and geraniol that have potent antifungal properties. These substances can help to reduce the growth of fungal infections that cause nail discoloration, brittleness, and crumbling. The antibacterial and anti-inflammatory agents in germander tea may also promote healthy nail growth, strengthening the nails and improving their overall appearance. Drinking germander tea regularly can be a natural and effective way to address nail fungus and achieve healthier, more attractive nails.
remedy_properties: Antifungal compounds: Germander tea contains antifungal compounds such as germanderin and limonene, which inhibit the growth of fungi that cause nail fungus, helping to prevent further infection.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in Germander tea, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to the development of nail fungus.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Germander tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as geranial and neral, soothe and calm irritated skin and nails, reducing redness and swelling associated with fungal infections.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, roots are often used in traditional medicine to treat fungal infections like nail fungus due to their antimicrobial properties.

Barks: The barks of the germander plant contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have been shown to exhibit antifungal activity against various types of fungi, making them effective for treating nail fungus.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried teucrium chamaedrys leaves and flowers in amounts of 20-30 grams for every 8 oz of water.
Combine the herbs with boiling water in a saucepan and steep for 10-15 minutes to release active compounds.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a large container discarding solids after 5 minutes.
Allow the tea to cool to room temperature then refrigerate for at least 2 hours before consumption within 24 hours.
Consume 1/2 cup of the prepared tea three times daily as needed until desired results are achieved.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder teas helps with nail fungus because of its potent antifungal properties, which target the root cause of the infection. The tea's high concentration of anthocyanins and flavonoids helps to reduce inflammation, promote healthy circulation, and boost the immune system, creating an environment that is less conducive to fungal growth. As a result, herbal elder teas can help to soothe and calm infected nails, promoting healthy nail growth and ultimately resolving fungal infections.
remedy_properties: Antifungal compounds: Elder tea contains antifungal compounds like flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungal pathogens that cause nail fungus.
Anti-inflammatory effects: Elder tea's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with nail fungus, promoting a healthier environment for nail growth.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in elder tea can help protect the nail bed from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the development of fungal infections.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used because they contain a high concentration of antifungal compounds that can help combat fungal infections causing nail fungus.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes, which are underground stems, are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe and protect the affected nails.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried sambucus nigra leaves and stems in the early morning when dew is still present.
Combine one teaspoon of dried sambucus nigra herb or two teaspoons of fresh sambucus nigra herb with boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 10 to 15 minutes allowing the herbal goodness to infuse into the water.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the solids before serving the warm tea immediately.
Drink three cups of the tea per day consistently for at least four weeks to support nail fungus treatment.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower teas helps with nail fungus because of its powerful antifungal properties. The herb, also known as Echinacea purpurea, contains compounds like cichoric acid and chicoric acid that have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungal cells. By incorporating purple coneflower tea into your daily routine, you can help reduce the appearance of nail fungus by promoting healthy nail growth and combating fungal infections. This natural remedy may also soothe inflammation and discomfort associated with fungal infections.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Purple coneflower tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like triterpenoids and flavonoids, which can help reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with fungal infections under the nails.

Antifungal properties: The tea's antifungal compounds, such as luteolin and kaempferol, have been shown to inhibit the growth of various fungal species, including those that cause nail fungus, by disrupting their cell membranes and inhibiting their metabolic pathways.

Antioxidant properties: Purple coneflower tea contains antioxidants like quercetin and isorhapontigenin, which can help protect the nails from oxidative damage caused by free radicals and promote healthy tissue repair and regeneration.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing fungal infections.
Rhyzomes: Contains saponins and terpenoids that help to inhibit fungal growth and promote healthy nail growth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh echinacea purpurea flowers in the morning after dew has dried for maximum potency.
Rinse one tablespoon of dried echinacea purpurea flowers or two tablespoons of fresh flowers with filtered water.
Steep the rinsed herbs in a cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release beneficial compounds.
Strain the tea into a new cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard solids after 10-15 minutes.
Drink one cup of cooled herbal tea three times daily for at least two months to alleviate nail fungus symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold teas helps with nail fungus because it contains flavonoids, which have potent antifungal properties that can inhibit the growth of fungal infections. The tea's anti-inflammatory compounds also reduce redness and swelling associated with nail fungal infections, promoting a healthy environment for new nail growth. Additionally, pot marigold's antimicrobial properties help to eliminate bacteria and fungi on the skin and nails, creating an ideal condition for treating and preventing nail fungus.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial activity: Pot marigold tea's antimicrobial properties help combat fungal infections by inhibiting the growth of Candida and other microorganisms that can cause nail fungus, allowing for healthy nail regrowth.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in pot marigold tea reduce swelling and discomfort associated with nail fungus, making it easier to manage symptoms and promote healing.

Antioxidant activity: Pot marigold tea's antioxidants help protect the nails from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the development of fungal infections and slow down the healing process.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the pot marigold contain antioxidants and antibacterial compounds that can help combat fungal infections causing nail fungus.
Seeds: The seeds are rich in anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with nail fungus.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried calendula officinalis flowers measuring one teaspoon per cup of boiling water.
Bring filtered water to a boil in a medium-sized teapot then reduce heat to simmering.
Add the dried calendula flowers to the tea leaves and steep for 10-15 minutes or until desired strength.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve into a large cup or mug removing the solids completely.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before soaking affected nails in it for up to 20 minutes daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow teas helps with nail fungus because of its antibacterial and antifungal properties, which help to combat the underlying infection that causes the condition. The tea's active compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, work together to reduce fungal growth and promote a healthy environment for nail regrowth. Additionally, yarrow tea's anti-inflammatory properties can help to soothe and calm the skin around the affected area, reducing redness and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Yarrow's antimicrobial compounds, such as thymol and carvacrol, help to combat fungal infections, including nail fungus, by inhibiting their growth and preventing them from spreading.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Yarrow's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling and redness around the affected nails, helping to alleviate discomfort and promote a healthy environment for healing.

Antioxidant properties: Yarrow's antioxidant compounds help to neutralize free radicals that can damage nail tissue, promoting healthy cell growth and reducing the risk of further infection or disease progression.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, yarrow roots help reduce fungal infections and promote healthy nail growth.
Leaves: High in flavonoids and phenolic acids, yarrow leaves have antifungal properties that inhibit the growth of fungi causing nail fungus.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried achillea millefolium leaves and flowers from a reputable source.
Combine 2 teaspoons of dried yarrow leaves and flowers with 8 ounces of boiling water in a heat-resistant cup.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, allowing the herbal goodness to infuse into the water.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove solids and discard them.
Drink the remaining tea 2-3 times daily, ensuring adequate hydration is maintained throughout treatment duration.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion teas helps with nail fungus because of its potent antifungal properties. The tea contains compounds like taraxasterol, which has been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi that cause nail infections. Additionally, dandelion's antioxidants and anti-inflammatory agents help to reduce inflammation and promote healthy tissue growth around the affected area. This natural remedy can be an effective alternative to traditional treatments for those seeking a more holistic approach to addressing fungal nail infections.
remedy_properties: Antifungal compounds: Dandelion tea contains antifungal compounds like taraxasterol, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungal organisms that cause nail fungus, helping to prevent infection and promote healthy nail growth.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in dandelion tea, such as sesquiterpenes, can help reduce inflammation and irritation around the affected area, making it less conducive to fungal growth and promoting a healthier environment for nail repair.

Antioxidant activity: Dandelion tea's high antioxidant content helps protect the nails from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the development of nail fungus. By neutralizing these free radicals, dandelion tea may help promote healthy, resilient nail growth.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the dandelion plant have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties, making them effective in treating fungal infections like nail fungus.
Seeds: Dandelion seeds contain a high concentration of fatty acids and antioxidants that can help to soothe and calm inflammation on the skin, reducing the symptoms of nail fungus.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried dandelion leaves and flowers in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Dry the harvested ingredients immediately using a food dehydrator on low heat for 2 hours.
Combine 2 tablespoons of dried dandelion leaves and flowers with 1 quart of boiling water in a large pot.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes to allow the herbs to release their active compounds into the water.
Strain the tea through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container for consumption within 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice teas helps with nail fungus because it contains a compound called glycyrrhizin, which has antifungal properties that can help combat fungal infections. The tea's antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory agents also promote healthy nail growth by reducing inflammation and preventing further infection. Additionally, licorice root has been shown to improve blood circulation, which is essential for delivering nutrients and oxygen to the nails, promoting overall health and strength. By drinking licorice tea regularly, individuals can help soothe and treat fungal infections, resulting in healthier and stronger nails.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Licorice root tea has been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi, including those that cause nail fungus, by disrupting their cell membranes and preventing them from multiplying.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in licorice root tea can help reduce swelling and redness associated with nail fungus, promoting a healthy environment for nail growth.
Antioxidant properties: Licorice root tea's high antioxidant content helps protect the body against free radicals that can damage healthy tissue and contribute to fungal infections.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties that help combat fungal infections like nail fungus.
Barks: Containing triterpenoid saponins, which exhibit antifungal and antibacterial activities, making them effective against fungal infections and promoting healthy nail growth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried glycyrrhiza glabra root extract containing 10-20% glycyrrhizinic acid.
Measure one teaspoon of the dried root extract and combine it with one cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant teapot or mug.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, allowing the active compounds to infuse into the water and release their bioactive properties.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove any solids and reserve the liquid for consumption.
Consume one cup of the herbal licorice tea three times daily, ideally before meals, for at least 60 days to promote nail health and combat fungal infections.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Three-lobed desert-holly teas helps with nail fungus because of its potent antimicrobial and antifungal properties. The tea's active compounds, such as terpenes and flavonoids, work to penetrate deep into the nail plate and surrounding tissues, effectively killing off fungal infections that cause discoloration, brittleness, and separation. Regular consumption of this herbal tea can help to strengthen the nails, promoting healthy growth and a clear appearance. As a natural remedy, it's an effective alternative to synthetic treatments for those seeking a holistic approach to managing nail fungus.
remedy_properties: Tea tree oil: Has antifungal and antibacterial properties, which can help combat fungal infections like nail fungus.
Echinacea: Contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help reduce inflammation and prevent infection in the affected area.
Goldenrod: Has antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and calm irritated skin and nails.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of this plant have been traditionally used to create antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties in tea, helping to combat fungal infections such as nail fungus.
Rhyzomes: With their rich store of bioactive compounds, rhyzomes are used to make tea that targets the underlying causes of fungal infections, promoting healthy nail growth.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried eriolobus trilobatus leaves and stems and place them in a clean glass jar.
Heat one quart of boiling water and let it cool down for 10-15 minutes before pouring it over the herb mixture.
Steep the herbs in hot water for 10-15 minutes or longer if you prefer a stronger tea.
Strain the tea into a separate container using a piece of cheesecloth or a fine-mesh strainer and discard the solids.
Drink 1/2 cup to 1 cup of the cooled tea 2-3 times daily for 6-8 weeks or as directed by your healthcare provider.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender teas helps with nail fungus because its antifungal properties provide a natural solution to combat this common issue. The tea's active compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungal pathogens that cause nail infections. By drinking English lavender tea regularly, individuals can help to reduce the severity of symptoms like discoloration, thickening, and brittleness associated with nail fungus, promoting healthier nails in the process.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Lavender tea's antimicrobial properties can help combat fungal infections, including nail fungus, by inhibiting the growth and replication of fungal spores.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender tea's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with nail fungus, promoting healthy nail growth and reducing symptoms such as redness and swelling.

Antioxidant properties: Lavender tea's antioxidant properties can help protect the nails from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the development of fungal infections.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help to reduce inflammation and promote healthy growth of nails.
Seeds: Lavender seeds contain fatty acids and terpenes that have antifungal properties, making them effective against fungal infections causing nail fungus.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried lavandula angustifolia flowers in late morning for maximum oil content and potent antifungal properties.
Combine one teaspoon of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers or three teaspoons of fresh petals with boiling water in a heat-resistant cup.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow the bioactive compounds like linalool and luteolin to infuse into the water.
Strain the tea leaves and discard solids, then add honey to taste and mix well to enhance the flavor and potential antibacterial effects.
Drink the tea three times a day for at least 2 weeks, with one cup after breakfast and two cups before bedtime, to promote healthy nail growth and fungal prevention.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's wort teas helps with nail fungus because of its antifungal properties, which specifically target and eliminate fungal infections. The tea's bioactive compounds, including hyperforin and hypericin, have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi that cause nail fungus, such as Candida and Aspergillus species. By promoting a healthy immune response and reducing inflammation, St John's wort teas create an environment that is less conducive to fungal overgrowth, allowing nails to grow strong and healthy once again.
remedy_properties: Hyperforin: This compound has been shown to have antifungal and antibacterial properties, which can help combat fungal infections such as nail fungus.
Flavonoids: The flavonoids present in St. John's Wort tea have antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the affected nails.
Phenolic acids: The phenolic acids in St. John's Wort tea have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungal cells, which can help slow down or prevent the spread of nail fungus.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of St John's Wort contain hyperforin, a compound that has been shown to have antifungal properties, making them effective in treating nail fungus.
Leaves: The leaves of St John's Wort are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help to reduce inflammation and promote healthy growth of the nails.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried st john's wort leaves and flowers measure two teaspoons.
Boil one quart of water in a pot then reduce heat to simmer.
Steep the st john's wort mixture in boiling water for 15-20 minutes allow.
Strain the tea using a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean cup discard.
Drink one cup of the tea three times daily after meals for 6-8 weeks regularly.

intro_desc: Herbal teas for nail fungus are a natural and effective way to treat fungal infections on the nails. These teas contain antioxidants, antibacterial, and antifungal properties that help to combat fungal growth and promote healthy nail growth. Examples of herbal teas that help with nail fungus include tea tree oil-infused tea, chamomile tea, and calendula tea. Drinking these teas regularly can help to improve nail health by reducing inflammation, promoting circulation, and providing essential nutrients. As a result, individuals with nail fungus can experience improved appearance, reduced discomfort, and increased confidence in their overall well-being.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat nail fungus with herbal teas is by combining different herbs that have antifungal properties. Ginger and tea tree oil-infused teas are effective in reducing fungal growth. You can also try drinking sage and lavender teas, which promote healthy circulation and nail growth. Mix 1-2 teaspoons of dried herbs with boiling water, steep for 5-7 minutes, and drink twice a day. Additionally, soak your affected nails in the herbal tea mixture for 15-20 minutes to enhance treatment effectiveness.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to nail fungus that are treated with herbal teas are skin conditions such as eczema, acne, and psoriasis. Herbal teas like chamomile, calendula, and lavender have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm irritated skin. Teas like green tea and turmeric also contain antioxidants that can reduce inflammation and promote healing in the skin.
